I think that's it, but let's just check and see if there's any, oh, okay. Hey, listen, this is kind of hard to say, but people are just using you when they put you on a burger to try to make it seem more healthy by including salad. asr_preview.text: We'll throw some oil in a pan on medium heat, then throw the onions in and wang jangle them occasionally until they look something like this. We're gonna grill our buns in some butter in advance so they're ready to go, which uses the exact same process as when you make a grilled butter sandwich.

For our burger sauce, I'm gonna take some mayo and hot sauce and wang jangle that together. I'm putting the sauce on both sides of the bun. Condimenting only one side of a bun is a certifiable burger crime. We need moisture coming from all directions, or at least two directions. We've got our cast iron griddle heating up, and you wanna crank that all the way up to medium. If you let it heat up for a full 10 minutes on medium, it's gonna be plenty hot. But if you wanna brag to your buddies later about how hot your pan is, go ahead and floor it.

claims.text: Using transcript evidence only for target identification, the most insulted ingredient is salad: the transcript explicitly says salad "get[s] soggy" and "taste[s] like nothing," while the other cited ingredient mentions are described as positive, neutral, descriptive, or not clearly insults.

Chronologically, the sampled visual candidates are 1-5s, 62-66s, and 107-110s. In the first sampled segment, salad is absent even though a cutting board is present. In the middle sampled segment, lettuce/salad is present but the cutting board is absent. In the last sampled segment, salad/lettuce is shown together with a cutting board.

Counting distinct appearances rather than raw frames, the sampled evidence supports one continuous late appearance of salad/lettuce with a cutting board across 107-110s, not three separate appearances. The earlier sampled segments do not qualify because either salad is absent or the cutting board is absent. However, because only three sampled windows were retrieved, the evidence does not establish that these are all appearances in the full video; additional unsampled appearances could change the count.

If the sampled windows were complete, one distinct qualifying appearance would map to option A. But the refinement rule says to leave the answer unresolved when the retrieved frame set may miss early or late candidate appearances. Since the evidence does not show full-video coverage, multiple answer choices remain compatible.

audit_gaps: whether the insulted ingredient appears with a cutting board in any unsampled parts of the video
the full-video total number of times the insulted ingredient is shown with a cutting board in the background
